page: Amazon Web Services m7a.metal-48xl is a bare-metal general-purpose server powered by AMD EPYC 9R14 processors. It features 192 physical cores and 192 vCPUs on the x86_64 architecture, operating at a clock speed of 3.7 GHz with a dedicated CPU allocation. The system is equipped with 768.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 5 GHz, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local NVMe storage or dedicated GPU hardware, but it delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier performance across multiple compute-intensive workloads. The server achieves top 10% placement in stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU evaluations, as well as Passmark CPU tests, including extended instructions, encryption, and physics simulations. Memory performance is highly competitive, with DDR5 memory bandwidth reaching top-tier speeds for cached and uncached read operations. In application-level tests, the server demonstrates exceptional throughput, processing 331.8 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks and delivering 113.03 GB/sec in static web serving.

The AMD EPYC processor supports advanced instruction sets, including AVX-512, AVX512_VNNI, and AVX512_BF16, which accelerate cryptographic operations and vector calculations. This is reflected in top-tier LLM inference prompt processing speeds across small, medium, and large models, though text generation speeds remain in the average to strong range. Given the high physical core density, dedicated CPU allocation, and substantial memory bandwidth, this bare-metal server is highly suited for large-scale database operations, software compilation, ray tracing, and high-throughput web serving.
description: Amazon Web Services m7a.metal-48xl is a bare-metal general-purpose server featuring 192 dedicated physical cores and 192 vCPUs powered by AMD EPYC 9R14 processors running at 3.7 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it includes 768.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory at 5 GHz, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. While it lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, it offers a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps. Benchmark results position this server in the top tier for multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, database operations, and static web serving. It supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 for cryptographic and vector workloads. The m7a.metal-48xl is optimized for demanding workloads such as large-scale database hosting, software compilation, ray tracing, and high-throughput web serving.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m7a.metal-48xl is a bare-metal server with 192 AMD EPYC cores, 768 GB DDR5 RAM, and 50 Gbps networking, delivering top-tier compute and database performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m7a.metal-48xl is a bare-metal server featuring 192 AMD EPYC cores, 768 GB DDR5 RAM, and top-tier multi-core performance.
tagline: A high-density bare-metal server featuring 192 dedicated AMD EPYC cores and high-speed DDR5 memory for demanding enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 192 dedicated AMD EPYC 9R14 physical cores running at 3.7 GHz
768.0 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory
Top-tier multi-core CPU, database, and static web serving performance
AVX-512 instruction support for accelerated vector and cryptographic tasks
Best suited for large-scale databases, software compilation, and web serving
